# Publisher

Source: `lib/protocols/proto/v1/publisher.proto`

## Services

### Publisher

#### Publisher.Capabilities

- Request: `Empty`
- Response: `PublisherCapabilities`

#### Publisher.Subscribe

- Request: `SubscribeRequest`
- Response: `SubscribeResponse`

#### Publisher.Unsubscribe

- Request: `UnsubscribeRequest`
- Response: `UnsubscribeResponse`

#### Publisher.ListSubscriptions

- Request: `Empty`
- Response: `ListSubscriptionsResponse`

## Messages

### PublisherCapabilities

PublisherCapabilities advertises the kinds this binary supports and
any mix-in protocols (e.g. "validation"). Rimsky reads it at
startup and caches in the discovery cache.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `supported_kinds` | `repeated PublisherKindCapability` | 1 |  |
| `protocols` | `repeated string` | 2 | protocols advertises mix-in service protocols (e.g. "publisher", "validation"). |
| `validation_supported_roles` | `repeated string` | 3 | validation_supported_roles: set when "validation" is in protocols (e.g. ["publisher"]). |

### PublisherKindCapability

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `kind` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `config_schema` | `bytes` | 2 | config_schema is the JSON Schema for the resolved_config the publisher accepts at Subscribe for this kind. Opaque to rimsky; surfaces to operator tooling. |

### SubscribeRequest

SubscribeRequest carries the publisher-subscription routing fields
inline (no on-observation substruct). The publisher uses target_node
and message_kind to build the message envelope at publish time.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `publisher_subscription_id` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `instance_id` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `kind` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `resolved_config` | `bytes` | 4 | resolved_config is the per-instance config the publisher watches against (e.g. cron expression, HTTP URL, S3 bucket+prefix). Substituted by rimsky from the template `publishers:` block before dispatch. |
| `target_node` | `string` | 5 | target_node is the receiver node alias on the instance side; the publisher copies this onto each message envelope as `target`. |
| `message_kind` | `string` | 6 | message_kind is the wire-level message kind (default "invalidate" when empty); the publisher copies this onto each message envelope as `kind`. |
